svg.title: Section 05 — What I'm Solving
svg.desc: Four problem statement cards with data callouts for the Inverse case study
svg.text: SECTION 05 — WHAT I'M SOLVING
THE PROBLEM SPACE
Four interconnected problems
that no existing product addresses together.
Framer: full-width section, dark background (#272A30). White text on dark.
This is the emotional centrepiece of the opening arc. Give it space — full viewport height.
DISTRUST IN FINANCIAL MARKETS
People don't distrust investing.
They distrust the systems around it.
Non-transparent advisory practices, conflicting social media signals, and a lack of regulatory
clarity create an environment where caution feels rational — even when it limits financial growth.
of Indians avoid
investing entirely
DEFICIT IN FINANCIAL LITERACY
Most adults — and the majority of women —
are navigating finances without the basics.
Financial illiteracy is not about intelligence. It's about access — to education, to trustworthy tools,
and to products designed for people who are just starting to understand their money.
of adults below RBI
literacy standard
STAGNATION IN INVESTMENT CHOICES
Conservative investing isn't a preference.
It's a default — in the absence of anything better.
56% of Indian households park savings in bank deposits. Not because it's optimal, but because
nothing else feels accessible, safe, or tailored to how they actually think about risk.
avg. real return
HINDERED FINANCIAL PROGRESS
Long-term goals exist. The tools
to act on them consistently don't.
Without personalised planning, most individuals fail to align their financial strategy with their life goals.
The gap between intention and action compounds over time — and is entirely preventable by design.
50Cr
Indians carry a loan
or credit card
These four problems don't exist in isolation.
Distrust keeps people out. Illiteracy keeps them stuck. Conservative defaults keep them underperforming.
And without planning tools, even motivated investors fail to close the gap between intention and action.
Inverse was designed to address all four — not as separate features, but as a single coherent experience.
FRAMER LAYOUT NOTES
Background: full-width dark section (#272A30). Cards sit on #1C2026 — slightly darker than the section bg.
Cards: stacked vertically, full width, 12px gap between. Left accent bar 4px wide, colour-coded per problem.
Data callout: top-right of each card. Number uses the same accent colour as the bar. Label in #9DA3AF.
Scroll animation: cards slide up one by one, 100ms stagger. On hover: accent bar extends full height smoothly.
Connecting statement below: white section background returns. No card wrapper — open prose.
